title: coreopsis paludosa
intro: Coreopsis paludosa, also known as marsh coreopsis or bog coreopsis, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, dyspepsia, and other gastrointestinal problems, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of Coreopsis paludosa typically involve infusions or decoctions of its roots and leaves, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, or topical creams for medicinal purposes.
